summary: R2 architecture review accepts M4-AUTO-ENGAGEMENT-SIEGE-001. The R2 fix adds parseSavedDomainEvent support for sim.m4-field-engagement-resolved and sim.m4-siege-choice-applied, adds save/load regression coverage for the new M4 combat event tail without modifying packages/save-format, and adds player/AI parity coverage for sim.apply-m4-siege-choice. Scope remains limited to deterministic automatic field engagement and the M4 siege choice loop through shared GameCommand paths.

tests_run.result: ## feat/m4-auto-engagement-siege; clean before this reviewer ACCEPT handoff was added.

Diff is limited to allowed protocol, sim-core, tests, project task/state, and project messages/routes paths. No forbidden client UI, desktop, save-format, content, docs/decisions, GitHub workflow, or product status paths were changed.
Node and Worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, and save/load canaries.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 31 root test files/212 tests passed; web package 1 file/3 tests passed.
Workspace typecheck passed for all packages with typecheck scripts.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse check for task and writer R2 handoff

tests_run.result: Not applicable: @monsoon/protocol has no package-level typecheck script. Root pnpm typecheck was run and passed.

decisions: ACCEPT: R2 fixes the prior P1 by adding sim-core parseSavedDomainEvent cases for sim.m4-field-engagement-resolved and sim.m4-siege-choice-applied, matching the event kinds already written by domainEventToRecord.
ACCEPT: R2 adds a focused save/load regression that round-trips a runtime eventTail containing both new M4 combat events; packages/save-format remains untouched.
ACCEPT: R2 adds sim.apply-m4-siege-choice player/AI parity coverage using invest-blockade plus assault and matching authoritative state hashes.
State mutation remains inside sim-core command evaluation paths, and player/AI actors share the same GameCommand semantics.
The implementation uses deterministic integer formulas, explicit reason/status codes, stable sorted canonicalization/read-model ordering, and no Math.random/Date.now authority.
Casualty and supply accounting is explicit in state and events, with world-state validation and tests covering nonnegative force/supply counts.
Protocol commands and siege read models remain explicit serializable payloads; package boundary checks passed.

No hard-excluded scope was observed: no manual node battle, tactical battle UI, 3D battle, complex naval battle, full diplomacy, peace treaties, multiplayer/server behavior, client UI, save-format changes, or product/architecture drift.
risks: R3 remains inherently active because the task changes authoritative sim-core world state, command/event/query protocol surfaces, and deterministic hash coverage; current evidence is sufficient for this milestone slice.
M4 world snapshot schema migration remains outside this task. This acceptance covers the required event-tail save/load compatibility only, with no packages/save-format changes.
requested_action: Proceed with lead orchestration for acceptance/closure of M4-AUTO-ENGAGEMENT-SIEGE-001.
